question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open many simultaneous TCP connections to port 5600 (each allowed by the absence of a per-IP connection cap in grpcServerConfigurer), saturate the shared applicationTaskExecutor thread pool across all connections, and cause 30%+ of gRPC mirror node instances to stop processing new requests?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer sets no maxConnectionAge on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing persistent long-lived connections that hold applicationTaskExecutor threads indefinitely, causing thread starvation across 30%+ of deployed gRPC nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxConnectionIdle configuration in grpcServerConfigurer to hold idle connections open indefinitely, exhausting the OS-level file descriptor limit (observed alert threshold: 80%) on 30%+ of gRPC nodes, causing them to reject all new connections?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open exactly maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection (default 5) long-running streaming calls per connection, then open thousands of connections without any per-IP or global connection limit enforced by grpcServerConfigurer, exhausting the applicationTaskExecutor on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the missing maxInboundMessageSize setting in grpcServerConfigurer to send a single oversized gRPC message that causes the Netty server to allocate unbounded heap memory, triggering an OOM condition that crashes 30%+ of gRPC processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of maxInboundMetadataSize in grpcServerConfigurer to send gRPC requests with extremely large header frames, causing Netty to buffer unbounded metadata and exhaust heap memory on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor (set via serverBuilder.executor) by submitting calls that block on slow database read transactions (via transactionOperationsReadOnly), starving the executor of threads and causing 30%+ of gRPC nodes to become unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger many concurrent read-only transactions via transactionOperationsReadOnly that each hold a database connection for the full statementTimeout (10 seconds), exhausting the HikariCP connection pool and causing 30%+ of gRPC nodes to queue all requests until OOM or timeout cascade?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer does not configure keepAliveTime or keepAliveTimeout on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing zombie connections to accumulate and consume Netty channel resources until 30%+ of nodes are unable to accept new connections?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a flowControlWindow setting in grpcServerConfigurer to send data faster than the server can process, causing Netty's internal buffer to grow unboundedly and exhaust heap memory on 30%+ of gRPC nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open connections and send partial HTTP/2 frames without completing the gRPC handshake, exploiting the missing handshakeTimeout in grpcServerConfigurer to hold Netty worker threads in a pending state and degrade 30%+ of gRPC nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer delegates all call execution to the shared applicationTaskExecutor without any bounded queue size limit, causing the executor's task queue to grow unboundedly under load and exhaust JVM heap memory on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger concurrent read-only transactions through transactionOperationsReadOnly that each acquire a database connection and then stall (e.g., via a slow query on a large topic), causing HikariCP pool exhaustion that propagates to 30%+ of gRPC nodes sharing the same database?
